title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best authentic Chicken Mole Sauce (Pollo en Mole) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Perfect for a flavorful Mexican dinner!
longTailKeywords: how to make chicken mole sauce
chicken mole sauce recipe from scratch
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Chicken Mole
Step-by-Step Instructions for Chicken Mole Sauce
Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Mole Sauce
Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
Variations and Substitutions for Mole Sauce
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of rich, dark authentic chicken mole sauce with tender chicken pieces, garnished with sesame seeds and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Master the art of Mexican cuisine with our authentic chicken mole sauce recipe, perfect for a flavorful Pollo en Mole.

description: Pollo en Mole is a truly iconic Mexican dish featuring tender chicken smothered in a rich, complex mole sauce. This recipe guides you through creating a balanced sauce with layers of savory, sweet, and smoky flavors. Its a celebration of traditional Mexican cooking perfect for any special occasion or a comforting family meal.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Place chicken pieces in a large pot with 4 cups of chicken broth or water. Add a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until chicken is cooked through and tender. Remove chicken and set aside, reserving the cooking liquid.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles and Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Briefly toast the dried ancho, mulato, and pasilla chiles for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant, being careful not to burn them. Remove chiles and place them in a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them soak for 20 minutes to soften. In the same skillet, add halved tomatoes, tomatillos, chopped onion, and garlic. Cook until softened and slightly charred, about 5-7 minutes. Add almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, raisins, and torn tortilla to the skillet and toast for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Drain the softened chiles. Combine the rehydrated chiles, toasted vegetables, nuts, seeds, raisins, and tortilla in a blender. Add the cinnamon stick, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and cloves. Pour in 1 cup of the reserved chicken cooking liquid. Blend until a very smooth paste forms. If necessary, add more chicken liquid a little at a time to achieve a thick, pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Cook the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended mole paste into the hot oil. Stir constantly to prevent sticking and spattering. Cook the mole for at least 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, allowing it to darken and thicken. This step is crucial for developing the deep flavors of the mole.

instructions.title: Add Chocolate and Simmer
instructions.text: Once the mole has cooked down and deepened in color, stir in the Mexican chocolate until it completely melts and is incorporated. Add another 1-2 cups of the reserved chicken cooking liquid, a little at a time, until the sauce reaches your desired consistency. It should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with salt to taste and add 1 teaspoon of sugar to balance the flavors.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken with Mole
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering mole sauce. Gently turn the chicken to coat evenly. Allow the chicken to simmer in the mole for an additional 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to heat through. Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of sesame seeds.

tips: For a truly authentic mole, toast your chiles and spices carefully to release their aromas without burning, which can make the sauce bitter.
The quality of your Mexican chocolate can significantly impact the final flavor. Look for brands specifically made for drinking or cooking, which often include cinnamon and other spices.
Mole sauce freezes beautifully. Make a larger batch and store portions in the freezer for quick and easy future meals. Thaw and reheat gently, adding a little chicken broth if needed.
article:
Pollo en Mole is more than just a dish; it’s a culinary journey through the heart of Mexico. Bursting with layers of flavor, this iconic Mexican chicken recipe showcases the incredible depth and complexity that defines the country's gastronomic heritage. At its core lies the legendary mole sauce, a rich, aromatic concoction that transforms simple chicken into an unforgettable feast. If you’ve ever been intimidated by the thought of making mole from scratch, fear not! While it’s a labor of love, the reward is a profoundly satisfying meal that will impress even the most discerning palates. This article will guide you through the process of creating an authentic Chicken Mole Sauce, sharing insights, tips, and serving suggestions to help you master this beloved Mexican classic.

## What is Chicken Mole Sauce?

The word "mole" (pronounced MOH-lay) comes from the Nahuatl word "molli," meaning "sauce" or "concoction." It refers to a family of incredibly intricate sauces, not just the chocolate-infused variety many people associate with it. While there are seven famous moles from Oaxaca alone (Negro, Rojo, Amarillo, Verde, Coloradito, Chichilo, and Manchamanteles), Mole Poblano is arguably the most renowned, and often the one used for Pollo en Mole.

The history of mole is as rich and complex as its flavor. Its origins trace back to pre-Hispanic times, with indigenous communities creating sophisticated sauces from chiles, seeds, and spices. With the arrival of the Spanish, new ingredients like nuts, dried fruits, and even chocolate were introduced, leading to the evolution of the mole we know today. Chicken Mole Sauce is a testament to this fusion of cultures, blending indigenous ingredients with European influences to create a truly unique flavor profile.

A traditional Chicken Mole Sauce is characterized by its remarkable complexity. It’s a symphony of flavors: earthy, smoky notes from various dried chiles; sweetness from fruits, plantains, and a touch of chocolate; nuttiness from almonds, peanuts, and sesame seeds; and warmth from an array of spices like cinnamon, cloves, and cumin. The chocolate, often unsweetened or very dark, doesn't make the sauce sweet but rather deepens its color and adds a subtle bitterness that balances the other strong flavors. The result is a thick, velvety sauce that clings beautifully to tender chicken, offering a dining experience that is both comforting and exhilarating.

## Ingredients You Need for Chicken Mole

Crafting an authentic Chicken Mole Sauce requires a diverse array of ingredients, each playing a crucial role in building its layered flavor profile. Don't be daunted by the long list; gathering them is half the adventure!

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   1 whole chicken (3-4 lbs), cut into 8 pieces, or 6-8 chicken thighs and drumsticks
    *   1 onion, halved
    *   2-3 cloves garlic
    *   Salt to taste
    *   Water to cover
*   **For the Chiles:**
    *   4-5 ancho chiles (for smoky, fruity notes)
    *   3-4 mulato chiles (for deeper, slightly sweet notes)
    *   2-3 pasilla chiles (for earthy, fruity notes)
    *   Optional: 1-2 chipotle chiles (for a touch of heat and smokiness)
*   **For the Aromatics & Vegetables:**
    *   1 medium white onion, roughly chopped
    *   4-5 cloves garlic, peeled
    *   2 medium ripe tomatoes, roughly chopped
    *   2-3 tomatillos, husked and quartered
    *   ½ plantain, sliced
*   **For the Nuts & Seeds:**
    *   ¼ cup blanched almonds
    *   ¼ cup peanuts
    *   2 tablespoons sesame seeds (plus more for garnish)
    *   1 tablespoon pumpkin seeds (pepitas)
*   **For the Spices & Thickeners:**
    *   1-2 tablespoons lard or vegetable oil
    *   1 small cinnamon stick
    *   3-4 whole cloves
    *   ½ teaspoon black peppercorns
    *   ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds
    *   2-3 star anise pods
    *   1-2 pieces of stale bread or a small handful of tortilla chips
*   **For the Sauce Base:**
    *   2-3 cups reserved chicken broth
    *   1-2 ounces Mexican chocolate (bitter, unsweetened, or very dark chocolate), chopped
    *   Salt and sugar to taste

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Chicken Mole Sauce

Making Chicken Mole Sauce is a process, but each step builds towards an extraordinary result.

**Step 1: Cook the Chicken**
Place the chicken pieces in a large pot with the halved onion, garlic cloves, and a generous pinch of salt. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and cooked through. Remove the chicken, strain and reserve 3-4 cups of the cooking broth, discarding the solids. Set both aside.

**Step 2: Prepare the Chiles**
Carefully remove the stems and seeds from the dried chiles. For a milder sauce, remove all the veins. Heat a dry comal or skillet over medium heat and toast the chiles for 30-60 seconds per side until fragrant but not burnt (burning makes them bitter). Place the toasted chiles in a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until soft.

**Step 3: Toast Nuts, Seeds, and Spices**
In the same dry skillet, toast the almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, and pumpkin seeds separately until lightly golden and fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Transfer to a plate. Next, lightly toast the cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, cumin seeds, and star anise until aromatic.

**Step 4: Sauté Aromatics and Thickeners**
In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the lard or oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and garlic and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the chopped tomatoes, tomatillos, and plantain slices, cooking until softened, about 8-10 minutes. Add the stale bread or tortilla chips and cook for another minute.

**Step 5: Blend Everything**
Drain the rehydrated chiles, reserving a little of the soaking liquid. In a blender, combine the softened chiles, sautéed aromatics (from Step 4), toasted nuts, seeds, and spices (from Step 3). Add about 1-2 cups of the reserved chicken broth and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. If the mixture is too thick, add a little more broth or chile soaking liquid. For an extra smooth sauce, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down to extract all the liquid, discarding any solids.

**Step 6: Cook the Sauce**
Heat 1-2 tablespoons of fresh lard or oil in the same pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended mole paste into the hot fat. This step is crucial for developing the deep flavor of the Chicken Mole Sauce. Cook, stirring constantly, for 15-20 minutes until the sauce darkens, thickens, and the oil separates slightly. Gradually stir in the remaining reserved chicken broth, a little at a time, until the sauce reaches your desired consistency (it should be thick enough to coat a spoon). Bring to a simmer.

**Step 7: Add Chocolate and Seasoning**
Reduce the heat to low, add the chopped Mexican chocolate, and stir until it is completely melted and incorporated. Season the Chicken Mole Sauce with salt and a pinch of sugar (if needed) to balance the flavors. Taste and adjust.

**Step 8: Combine Chicken and Sauce**
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering mole sauce. Spoon the sauce over the chicken to coat thoroughly. Simmer gently for another 15-20 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the chicken to heat through.

## Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Mole Sauce

Achieving an exceptional Chicken Mole Sauce involves a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Quality Ingredients are Key:** Use fresh, high-quality dried chiles. Seek out authentic Mexican chocolate, which is typically unsweetened or very dark and often contains cinnamon.
*   **Don't Rush the Toasting:** Toasting chiles, nuts, and spices develops their flavors significantly. Do each component separately, as they toast at different rates. Watch them carefully to prevent burning, which can make your sauce bitter.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** A smooth mole sauce is a hallmark of authenticity. Use a high-powered blender. If your blender isn't super strong, or if you prefer an exceptionally fine texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve.
*   **The "Frying" of the Sauce:** The step where you cook the blended mole paste in hot fat (Step 6) is absolutely essential. This process, often called "freír la salsa," deepens the color and flavor, transforming the raw paste into a rich, complex sauce. Don't skip or shorten this step.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Mole is all about balance. Taste your Chicken Mole Sauce frequently and adjust seasoning. A pinch of salt can brighten flavors, and a touch of sugar can balance any bitterness from the chiles or chocolate. A squeeze of lime juice at the end can also add a fresh lift.
*   **Make Ahead:** Mole sauce actually tastes better the next day, as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen. Prepare the sauce a day in advance, then gently reheat and add the chicken just before serving.
*   **Handle Chiles Safely:** Wear gloves when deseeding and handling dried chiles to avoid skin irritation. Wash your hands thoroughly afterwards.

## Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole

Pollo en Mole is a showstopper on its own, but traditional accompaniments elevate the experience.

*   **Rice:** Serve it alongside a generous portion of classic Mexican red rice or simple white rice to soak up every drop of that luscious sauce.
*   **Tortillas:** Warm corn tortillas are a must! They are perfect for scooping up the chicken and sauce, making for a truly authentic bite. Flour tortillas also work if preferred.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds over the finished dish adds a lovely texture and visual appeal. Chopped fresh cilantro or a crumble of queso fresco can also be nice garnishes.
*   **Beans:** A side of refried beans complements the richness of the mole beautifully.
*   **Salad:** A simple, crisp green salad with a light vinaigrette can offer a refreshing contrast to the hearty mole.
*   **Drinks:** Pair with a refreshing agua fresca, horchata, a Mexican lager, or even a dry red wine like a Tempranillo.

## Variations and Substitutions for Mole Sauce

While the classic Chicken Mole Sauce is a masterpiece, there's room for personalization and adaptation.

*   **Protein Alternatives:** While chicken is traditional, mole is also excellent with other proteins. Consider turkey for a festive holiday meal, pork shoulder (slow-cooked until tender), duck, or even beef short ribs. For a vegetarian version, use roasted sweet potatoes, mushrooms, or firm tofu.
*   **Chile Customization:** Adjust the heat and flavor profile by varying the types and quantities of chiles. For more heat, add a de-seeded arbol chile or a small amount of habanero. For a smokier flavor, increase the amount of chipotle chiles.
*   **Nut & Seed Adjustments:** If you have allergies or preferences, you can swap out nuts and seeds. Pecans, cashews, or sunflower seeds can be used in place of or in addition to almonds and peanuts.
*   **Sweeteners:** Instead of granulated sugar, traditional piloncillo (Mexican brown sugar cones) can be used for a deeper, more nuanced sweetness.
*   **Chocolate Ratio:** Some mole recipes use more chocolate, others less. Experiment to find your preferred balance. You can also use a combination of dark and milk chocolate for a slightly sweeter finish, though traditional mole uses unsweetened.
*   **Time-Saving Shortcuts:** For those short on time, good quality pre-made mole paste (found in Mexican grocery stores) can be a convenient starting point. While it won't replicate the depth of a homemade Chicken Mole Sauce, it provides a flavorful base that can be enhanced with fresh broth and additional spices.

Pollo en Mole is a dish that embodies the soul of Mexican cuisine – rich, complex, and utterly satisfying. While it demands time and attention, the process of creating this exquisite Chicken Mole Sauce from scratch is a rewarding culinary adventure. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to embark on a flavorful journey that culminates in a meal you'll be proud to share.
